steps.title: Why are you here?
steps.description: Turn broad curiosity into a clear starting path by identifying the kind of value you want this academy to create for you.

questions.prompt: What do you most want from Personal Academy right now?

options.label: Build a stronger model of reality
options.description: You want clarity, first principles, and better mental models.

options.label: Understand how civilization actually works
options.description: You care about systems, infrastructure, technology, and operations.

options.label: Navigate people, institutions, and society better
options.description: You want judgment around politics, incentives, communication, and group behavior.

options.label: Become wiser and more inwardly grounded
options.description: You want depth, reflection, and a clearer relationship to yourself.

options.label: Move toward real-world action and synthesis
options.description: You want the academy to shape practical identity, projects, and outcomes.

questions.prompt: What would make this system feel genuinely valuable to you?

options.label: I can explain the world more clearly
options.description: You want clearer reasoning and fewer blind spots.

options.label: I can make better decisions in work and life
options.description: You want the academy to improve judgment, not just knowledge.

options.label: I can connect knowledge to real projects
options.description: You want the system to produce practical capability.

options.label: I can feel more coherent and grounded
options.description: You want a calmer and more integrated relationship to life and learning.

steps.title: What do you already know?
steps.description: Map what feels familiar so the first blueprint starts at the right level.

questions.prompt: Which areas already feel familiar to you?

options.label: Science and math
options.description: You already have some comfort with formal reasoning.

options.label: Business and money
options.description: You already think in terms of value, risk, and tradeoffs.

options.label: Politics and history
options.description: You already have some map of institutions, conflict, and change over time.

options.label: Technology and software
options.description: You already know some of the technical stack of modern systems.

options.label: Reflection and inner work
options.description: You already value introspection, awareness, and personal coherence.

questions.prompt: How do you prefer your study to feel?

options.label: Strict structure and clear steps
options.description: You want the path to be explicit and ordered.

options.label: A mix of structure and freedom
options.description: You want enough guidance to stay moving, but still room to explore.

options.label: Open exploration
options.description: You want discovery and breadth before strict sequencing.

options.label: Learning by doing
options.description: You want the academy to behave like an operating environment.

steps.title: How do you learn best?
steps.description: Capture your cognitive style so the system can adapt the shape of guidance.

questions.prompt: Which learning style feels most natural to you?

options.label: Concepts and theory first
options.description: You like the model before the application.

options.label: Examples and case studies
options.description: You want reality to anchor the concepts.

options.label: Diagrams, maps, and structure
options.description: You like systems rendered visually and spatially.

options.label: Projects and practical exercises
options.description: You learn best when the work demands action.

options.label: Reflection and synthesis
options.description: You want space to integrate ideas and notice what changes in your thinking.

questions.prompt: What level of depth do you want when the system teaches you something new?

options.label: Fast orientation first
options.description: Give me the core idea quickly so I can move.

options.label: Balanced explanation
options.description: Give me enough detail to understand, but keep it usable.

options.label: Deep, full treatment
options.description: I want the long explanation, including edge cases and tradeoffs.

questions.prompt: Describe a time you learned something deeply and it changed how you saw the world.
questions.placeholder: A few sentences is enough. Optional, but useful for tuning the blueprint voice.

steps.title: How much time and rhythm can you sustain?
steps.description: Use realistic weekly availability to shape a path you will actually keep using.

questions.prompt: How much learning time can you realistically sustain each week?

options.label: Under 2 hours
options.description: Keep it light and frictionless.

options.label: 2 to 5 hours
options.description: Enough for steady progress without overload.

options.label: 5 to 10 hours
options.description: You can sustain a serious path and a real project rhythm.

options.label: 10+ hours
options.description: You can run an intensive blueprint with deeper projects and reviews.

questions.prompt: What rhythm would you actually keep using?

options.label: Short daily sessions
options.description: A little every day keeps the path warm.

options.label: A few longer sessions each week
options.description: You prefer depth over frequency.

options.label: Flexible exploratory sessions
options.description: You want to follow curiosity without a rigid calendar.

options.label: Scheduled deep work blocks with projects
options.description: You want the system to drive concrete output.

questions.prompt: How quickly do you want the system to move you into application?

options.label: Very quickly
options.description: I want projects and real-world use right away.

options.label: After some foundation work
options.description: I want enough structure before I start applying it.

options.label: Only once I understand the map
options.description: I want depth before I move to application.

options.label: I want both theory and application from the start
options.description: Give me the model and the use-case together.

steps.title: What roles pull at you?
steps.description: Use real-world role interest to bias the blueprint toward synthesis and application.

questions.prompt: Which roles are most interesting to you right now?

options.label: Astronaut
options.description: Systems, endurance, technical competence, and frontier living.

options.label: AI Researcher
options.description: Mathematics, experimentation, and frontier systems.

options.label: Robotics Engineer
options.description: Hardware, software, control, and real-world systems.

options.label: Pilot
options.description: Judgment, systems thinking, and operating in uncertainty.

options.label: Intelligence Analyst
options.description: Ambiguity, evidence, judgment, and geopolitical patterns.

options.label: Diplomat
options.description: Negotiation, statecraft, language, and long-horizon power.

options.label: Entrepreneur / Startup Founder
options.description: Building things from nothing and making tradeoffs in the real world.

questions.prompt: Which role lens sounds most useful for your life right now?

options.label: Technical and systems-oriented
options.description: You want engineering-style thinking and real mechanisms.

options.label: Strategic and people-oriented
options.description: You want judgment around incentives, power, and coordination.

options.label: Reflective and meaning-oriented
options.description: You want introspection, coherence, and clarity of values.

options.label: Practical and action-oriented
options.description: You want the system to push you toward real output.

steps.title: What kind of worldview are you building?
steps.description: Clarify the deeper direction so the academy can prioritize the right truths and the right cadence.

questions.prompt: Which broad direction matters most to you?

options.label: Reality and first principles
options.description: You want a cleaner, truer model of how the world works.

options.label: Civilization, institutions, and systems
options.description: You want to understand how large human systems are built and managed.

options.label: People, politics, and power
options.description: You want to read behavior, institutions, and incentives more accurately.

options.label: Meaning, consciousness, and wisdom
options.description: You want coherence, self-knowledge, and a more thoughtful inner life.

options.label: A synthesis of all of the above
options.description: You want breadth that becomes practical capability.

questions.prompt: What kind of truth sources do you trust most?

options.label: Primary sources, papers, and direct evidence
options.description: You value source quality and directness.

options.label: Established institutions and serious practitioners
options.description: You trust lived expertise and long-standing institutions.

options.label: Writers and interpreters with judgment
options.description: You want depth and synthesis from trusted explainers.

options.label: Real-world results and operational usefulness
options.description: You care most about what works in practice.
